sections.paragraph:
ἐκτυπόω fut. 2 sg. ἐκτυπώσεις. Pass.: aor. ἐξετυπώθην; pf. ptc. ἐκτετυπωμένος LXX (s. τύπος; Hippoc., Pla., X. et al.; LXX; JosAs 3:10; EpArist 98; Philo, Somn. 2, 191; Mel., Fgm. 12, 8) to follow a pattern in forming someth., to shape εἰς τ. μορφὴν τούτων ἐκτυπωθῆναι be shaped in their form Dg 2:3 (Cos. and Dam. 13, 23 ἐν ᾧ ἐκτυποῦνται σχήματι).—DELG s.v. τύπτω.

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. sg. = singular

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pf. = perfect

ptc. ptc. = participle

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

someth. someth. = something

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 311
